mission.title: envoy: Expose stats api to lua filter scripts
mission.description: Expose stats api to lua filter scripts. Requested by 41+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
*Title*: Expose stats api to lua filter scripts

*Description*: It would be great if lua filter scripts could somehow hook into the envoy stats infrastructure so that, for instance, they could increment custom counters based on conditions

steps.title: Apply the fix for Expose stats api to lua filter scripts
steps.description:
it was a local branch with everything hacked up, so I didn't push it.
```yaml
metrics.inc(request_handle, "my.metric.name", { "my_tag:" .. tag_value })
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Expose stats api to lua filter scripts" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: it was a local branch with everything hacked up, so I didn't push it.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
